skill.name: slack-gif-creator
skill.description: Create animated GIFs optimized for Slack using local Python utilities for frame composition, easing, validation, and file size reduction.
skill.summary: Build and validate Slack-ready animated GIFs with local image processing utilities.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium indicators, but the reviewed context shows they are false positives from animation terminology, Markdown examples, and Apache license text. No evidence found of malicious network access, command execution, prompt injection, credential handling, or obfuscated code. The skill performs expected local image and GIF file processing.

low_findings.title: Dismissed C2 Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword findings are caused by local animation constants named c1, c2, and c3 inside easing formulas. They do not perform command and control behavior, networking, persistence, or data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding code is a deterministic easing formula for animation timing. There is no network API, process execution, or encoded payload near these lines.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are text matches inside descriptions, documentation, and Apache license wording. The reviewed lines do not import or call cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matches are natural-language occurrences in comments, metadata, or license text. No hashing, encryption, signing, or password storage behavior is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown External Command Matches
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Python and bash examples inside fenced Markdown blocks. They document local GIF generation and dependency installation rather than hidden command execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are Markdown code fences and inline code spans. The only shell example installs declared image libraries and does not download or execute remote scripts.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Dynamic Import Match
low_findings.description: The dynamic import finding is a Python import statement shown in documentation for frame helper functions. It is not a JavaScript dynamic import expression or runtime loader.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The snippet statically imports named helpers from core.frame_composer. No variable-controlled module name or remote module source is used.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Network URL Matches
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are Apache License reference URLs. They are not used by runtime code and do not transmit user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are standard license text. No code references these URLs, and no HTTP client is present in the reviewed runtime files.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are ordinary documentation and function text about creating frames and helper utilities. They do not inspect hostnames, users, processes, environment variables, or system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines describe image-frame helpers and skill limitations. There are no reconnaissance APIs such as platform inspection, shell commands, or environment enumeration.

content.value_statement: Slack GIFs often fail because dimensions, duration, or file size are wrong. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to build and validate optimized animated GIFs locally.
actual_capabilities: Creates GIF frames with Pillow drawing primitives and helper functions.
Builds animated GIF files from generated or uploaded image frames.
Optimizes GIF dimensions, colors, frame count, and duplicate frames for Slack.
Validates GIF size, dimensions, frame count, duration, and Slack readiness.
Provides easing functions for bounce, elastic, back, and linear motion.
Documents Slack-specific GIF recommendations for emoji and message use.
limitations: Does not include prebuilt character art, icons, or animation templates.
Uses local image processing and cannot publish GIFs to Slack by itself.
Emoji font rendering is not reliable across platforms and is discouraged.
Output quality depends on the requested concept and available source images.

use_cases.title: Team Emoji Creation
use_cases.description: Create small looping emoji GIFs that meet Slack dimension and color limits.

use_cases.title: Internal Announcement Animations
use_cases.description: Generate short message GIFs for launches, milestones, or recurring reminders.

use_cases.title: Design Prototype Motion
use_cases.description: Prototype simple motion ideas with easing, bounce, pulse, slide, and particle concepts.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Emoji GIF
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new Slack emoji.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 128x128 Slack emoji GIF of a smiling sun that pulses gently for two seconds.

prompt_templates.title: Animate an Uploaded Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a user-provided image as the source.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use my uploaded logo as the base and make a Slack-ready GIF with a subtle bounce and sparkle effect.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize an Existing GIF
prompt_templates.scenario: Reduce size and validate Slack readiness.
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize this GIF for Slack emoji use. Reduce file size, keep the loop readable, and validate the final result.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Custom Motion Concept
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 480x480 Slack message GIF with a rocket launch, easing-based motion, exhaust particles, and final size recommendations.

output_examples.input: Create a pulsing heart emoji for Slack.
output_examples.output: A 128x128 looping GIF with a red heart, smooth pulse timing, reduced colors, and Slack emoji validation notes.

output_examples.input: Make this uploaded badge bounce and sparkle.
output_examples.output: A Slack-ready GIF that uses the badge image, adds a bounce cycle, overlays small sparkles, and reports dimensions and file size.

output_examples.input: Shrink my announcement GIF for Slack.
output_examples.output: An optimized GIF with fewer frames, a reduced color palette, duplicate-frame removal, and guidance for further size reduction.

best_practices: Keep emoji GIFs near 128x128 pixels and under three seconds.
Use fewer colors and frames when Slack upload size matters.
Validate the final GIF before sharing it with a workspace.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on platform emoji fonts for important visual details.
Do not create long loops when a short reaction animation will work.
Do not skip validation after resizing or optimizing frames.

faq.question: Can this skill upload GIFs directly to Slack?
faq.answer: No. It creates and validates local GIF files, but Slack upload is a separate manual step.

faq.question: Does it work with uploaded images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explains how to load uploaded images with Pillow and use them directly or as visual references.

faq.question: What size should Slack emoji GIFs use?
faq.answer: The skill recommends 128x128 pixels for emoji GIFs and short loops under three seconds.

faq.question: Can it reduce large GIF files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can reduce dimensions, colors, frames, and duplicate frames to lower file size.

faq.question: Does it include ready-made artwork?
faq.answer: No. It provides drawing helpers, animation concepts, and GIF utilities rather than a built-in art library.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
